caption:
Garantire agli uomini e alle donne del continente africano una libertà spesso negata: quella di scegliere di restare nella propria Nazione per contribuire alla sua crescita, senza essere costretti a lasciarla, pagando spesso trafficanti senza scrupoli e rischiando la vita nel tentativo di attraversare il Mediterraneo.

Dal mio intervento all’Assemblea dei Capi di Stato e di Governo dell’Unione Africana.
text: Italy intends to continue to be a privileged bridge between Europe and Africa, making available the strength of its institutions, its great tradition of dialogue, and the expertise of its companies. We want to do this also to achieve another goal that we all consider momentous, to guarantee the men and women of this continent a freedom that has often been denied them. The freedom to choose to remain in their country, to contribute to its growth without being forced to leave it often paying unscrupulous traffickers to risk their lives in an attempt to cross the Mediterranean. Those who believe that migration is necessary and indispensable are in fact acting selfishly. If young people leave their land and their people in pursuit of the promise of a better life, what will become of the history, culture, and existence of a country they have abandoned? These words are not mine, nor those of a political leader, but of a son who is proud of Africa and who holds Africa's future close to his heart, which is the Guinean Cardinal, Robert Sarach. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you.
